behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between availability filtering and ranking ("excluded ... regardless of relevance score"). A pure unit test of scorer or filter alone can miss reintroduction during pipeline wiring. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess, AI model output, or multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Wire recommender service with in-memory catalog + ranking module. Seed one high-score out-of-stock item and several lower-score in-stock items; assert response never includes out-of-stock IDs.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is category-block settings handoff into filtering, not just local predicate logic. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: text only specifies settings-driven exclusion, with no real infra or model-output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create user settings fixture with blocked categories, run recommendation request through service pipeline, and assert returned products contain none of the blocked categories.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets ranking logic itself (scoring preference by browsing signal), which is one algorithm boundary and does not require module wiring or I/O. Rejected Integration+: no behavior text about cross-module handoff, infra, or AI model dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Call ranking/scoring function with controlled feature vectors where only browsing-signal differs; assert items with recent-browsing match receive higher score/rank.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at ordering-to-pagination boundary (stable ordering across repeated page fetches). Unit-testing paginator alone can miss instability introduced by upstream sorter/output contract. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: no required real OS/network/model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommender pipeline on fixed dataset, request page 2 twice with identical user state and snapshot, assert same ordered IDs and correct page window each time.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: This is an algorithmic output constraint (serendipity rule) within ranking/selection logic. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not require external infra or non-deterministic model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Feed algorithm a user profile and candidate set with both seen and unseen categories; assert top-N output contains at least one unseen-category product.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: Cold-start behavior depends on orchestration between history lookup (empty) and fallback popularity source. A unit test of fallback function alone can miss miswired branching. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: no real OS/network requirement or AI-output dependence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke recommendation service for user with empty history repositories; assert non-empty results and that ranking follows popularity signal fixture.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is event handoff from recommendation response/click handler into tracking persistence (ID, position, timestamp contract). Unit tests of logger object alone can miss mapping errors. Rejected System: behavior does not require real network/subprocess/OS primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Serve a recommendation list, simulate click on a known position, then read tracking store and assert product_id, rank_position, and timestamp fields are persisted correctly.

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: Performance threshold is an infra-sensitive behavior; in-process mocks can hide real CPU/memory/serialization/query costs. Real process/runtime conditions are the failure boundary. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI model or multi-agent sequence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommender as real service process with a 100k-product dataset in realistic local storage, execute end-to-end recommendation requests, and assert latency target (e.g., p95 < 200ms).

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is state propagation across modules: purchase event updates user state, then next recommendation reflects deprioritization. Unit-testing scoring alone misses event-to-state handoff defects. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: text does not require real OS/network or model-output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Generate initial recommendations, mark one recommended product as purchased through purchase module, request recommendations again, and assert that product rank decreases (or is removed) per policy.

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Determinism here is a pipeline-level contract across feature extraction, ranking, and ordering under unchanged state; unit tests on scorer alone can miss non-determinism from module composition (e.g., unstable tie ordering). Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: behavior text does not require real OS/network or LLM/non-deterministic model output.
behaviors.test_description: Freeze user/profile/catalog state, run two full recommendation calls through service pipeline, and assert identical ordered product IDs and scores.
